news.title: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
news.summary: Meta announced Monday that it's rolling out a wave of new AI features on Facebook, the latest sign of the company's effort to catch up in the AI race and keep users more engaged on the platform.

news.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
news.summary: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and maybe some who won't), pre-IPO deals, and what's tucked inside its S-1 registration document.

news.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
news.summary: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

news.title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
news.summary: A group made up of dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to remove export control restrictions on Anthropic’s models Fable and Mythos, arguing that the order is going to limit the ability of cybersecurity defenders to secure their software and products.

news.title: SpaceX’s biggest-ever IPO just grew to $85.7 billion raised
news.summary: SpaceX's IPO underwriters maxed out their share purchases, adding to an already historic amount of money raised.

news.title: Fox wants to take over your TV — and the tech inside it
news.summary: Fox is about to take over the TVs in more than 100 million homes worldwide. On Monday, Fox announced that it's acquiring Roku, the streaming middleman that serves as a portal for viewers to hop into services like Netflix, Disney Plus, Hulu, and more. news.title: Amazon’s Smart Thermostat is on sale for just $58
news.summary: If your electricity bill climbs every summer, a smart thermostat could help keep cooling costs in check. news.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews
news.summary: Critics say bans push kids to riskier alternatives and can be beaten with VPNs.

news.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
news.summary: Debt sale set to test investor appetite for further exposure to AI sector amid a deluge of borrowing.

news.title: A Chinese rocket breaks apart dangerously close to the Starlink constellation
news.summary: The rocket's breakup likely generated 100 to 150 new pieces of space junk.

news.title: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
news.summary: Fox plans to take over Roku's streaming hardware, OS, and FAST services.

news.title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
news.summary: AMD's stripping of TSME from consumer CPUs appears to be a deliberate, covert move.

news.title: TCL A65K Soundbar Review: Small Size, Big Sound
news.summary: Don’t be fooled by the compact size of this soundbar. It’s a solid option for smaller TVs or spaces without having to sacrifice sound quality.

news.title: The Kratom Civil War Is Heating Up, and MAHA Has Picked a Side
news.summary: Both kratom and one of its active components, 7-OH, have opioid-like effects and are widely available across the US. As health secretary RFK Jr. aims to get 7-OH banned, proponents of both are fighting.

news.title: How Apple Is Making Your Older iPhone Run Faster and Stay Alive Longer
news.summary: Even the aging iPhone 11 will feel a little more responsive soon, thanks to improvements in an unsung iOS feature.

news.title: 1 in 4 World Cup Matches Could Be Played in Dangerous Temperatures
news.summary: A new report warns that Miami, Kansas City, Philadelphia, Dallas, and Houston could be particularly hot places to play during the 2026 World Cup.

news.title: A Crypto Scam Targeted a Gay OnlyFans Star. Then His X Feed Was Flooded With ‘MAGA Propaganda’
news.summary: In recent months hackers have attempted to extort money from porn stars with big followings, in some cases filling their feeds with pro-MAGA and crypto content.
